route.name: Redwood Backcountry Rambler
route.region: Redwood Coast / Smith River / Crescent City
route.description: Official/public GPX overnighter with paved backroads, forest roads, rooty/rugged singletrack, and creek decisions.
route.surfaceMix: Official/public GPX overnighter with paved backroads, forest roads, rooty/rugged singletrack, and creek decisions.
route.bestSeason: Mostly March to November; refresh Redwood NPS current conditions, creek crossing, fire, and forecast.

difficultyProfile.surface: Official/public GPX overnighter with paved backroads, forest roads, rooty/rugged singletrack, and creek decisions.
difficultyProfile.traffic: review paved connectors and trailhead access
difficultyProfile.technical: moderate unless source notes say otherwise

resources.water: NPS says Little Bald Hills water must be packed in. Use the GPX water milepoints only to support an explicit carry/filter plan; treat camp as dry.
resources.food: No food on route; carry all meals.
resources.camping: Little Bald Hills backcountry camp is the intended overnight. NPS says the camp is open and bike-accessible, but the actual July 11 online permit must be confirmed before gate-confirmed.
resources.toiletsShowers: Use mapped waypoints and land-manager pages; confirm current operation before departure.
resources.bikeShops: Identify nearest shops and exact hours during final trip refresh.
resources.longestGap: Operational gap is not just water-point spacing: it is the final reliable filter/carry point to dry camp plus overnight. Do not promote until the carry volume is chosen.
resources.mosquitoes: Seasonal; check recent reports for camp and water-adjacent sections.

tips: Most credible short overnighter if you can get the Little Bald Hills permit.
Carry or filter water; camp water is seasonal/limited and not treated.
Expect rooty/rugged singletrack rather than a casual path ride.
Best short source-route candidate once Little Bald Hills permit is secured.
Use wider tires and expect actual trail riding.
Check NPS current conditions immediately before travel.

verification.notes: Public GPX imported and parsed locally. Final legal/resource checks still control gate confirmation.

mapQuality.reviewRule: Route line is imported from public GPX and suitable for planning review.

review.decision: promote to plan_ready source coverage only; gate status remains blocked until date-specific gates pass

requiredFixes: Obtain/confirm Little Bald Hills permit for July 11.
Refresh NPS current conditions, Bald Hills/Howland Hill road notes, fire/smoke/AQI, and Crescent City/Hiouchi forecast within 24-48 hours of departure.
Confirm and record actual Little Bald Hills permit for July 11.
Finalize dry-camp water plan: carry overnight water or filter before camp; do not rely on water at Little Bald Hills.
Refresh NPS conditions, Howland Hill/Bald Hills Road, fire restrictions, AQI/smoke, and Crescent City/Hiouchi forecast on July 8-10.
Obtain and record the actual Little Bald Hills backcountry permit confirmation for the July 11 overnight.
Record the explicit overnight water plan: carry all camp water or identify the final reliable filter/carry point before Little Bald Hills.
Refresh NPS current conditions, Bald Hills Road and Howland Hill Road status, CAL FIRE incidents, AirNow/smoke, and NWS forecasts on July 8-10 before departure.
Open the Redwood Parks Conservancy scheduler for Little Bald Hills and capture or hold the July 11 permit, or contact Redw_Permits@nps.gov for confirmation.
Obtain and record the actual Little Bald Hills backcountry permit confirmation for the night of July 11, 2026.
Record a dry-camp water plan with explicit carried volume or final filter/top-off point before Little Bald Hills.
Refresh NPS current conditions, CAL FIRE/InciWeb, AirNow or fire.airnow.gov, and NWS forecasts for Crescent City/Hiouchi/Little Bald Hills on July 8-10.
For Gate Confirmed, overlay the GPX against official access and closure sources.
For Gate Confirmed, choose exact overnight, water/resupply, bailout, and parking plan.
For Gate Confirmed, refresh weather, smoke, fire, and road/park alerts close to departure.
openQuestions: What changed in the July 9-10 final refresh for closures, weather, smoke, water, and camps?

promotionChecklist.targetWindowNote: The 22.4-mile, 2-day route can fit inside July 10-13, 2026 if ridden July 11-12, with July 10 as arrival/check day and July 13 as buffer.
promotionChecklist.officialGeometryNote: Public route page and GPX exist for Redwood Backcountry Rambler.
promotionChecklist.campingPermitsNote: NPS requires an online backcountry permit for overnight camping, and public sources do not confirm an issued or available Little Bald Hills permit for the July 11, 2026 overnight.
promotionChecklist.closuresFireWeatherNote: Howland Hill Road and Bald Hills Road were open in current conditions, but Bald Hills has hazards/construction delays and NPS says conditions can change quickly; fire/smoke/AQI/weather must be refreshed July 8-10.
promotionChecklist.legalAccessNote: NPS identifies Little Bald Hills Trail as a suggested bike route and Little Bald Hills Camp as designated for hikers, bicycles, horses, and pack animals; no e-bikes on Little Bald Hills Trail.
promotionChecklist.waterResupplyNote: NPS says Little Bald Hills has no treated water; pack in water or bring a filter/purifier, with only seasonal non-potable water at camp.
promotionChecklist.mapArtifactsNote: Existing imported GPX/map artifacts remain adequate for Plan Ready use.

days.name: Redwood Backcountry Rambler segment 1
days.camp: Little Bald Hills backcountry camp requires a permit for the actual overnight date.

waypoints.name: Bikes not permitted on these trails. We parked and walked.

logistics: Official NPS Redwood backcountry page: all backcountry camping requires an online permit, requested up to 160 days in advance and no later than 24 hours before the trip. https://www.nps.gov/redw/planyourvisit/backcountry.htm

Official NPS current conditions page updated June 25, 2026 lists Little Bald Hills backcountry camp open and says backcountry permits are issued online only. https://www.nps.gov/redw/planyourvisit/conditions.htm

NPS current conditions page says Bald Hills Road is open with hazard conditions/road construction and possible 20-30 minute delays near Lady Bird Johnson Grove to Redwood Creek Overlook. https://www.nps.gov/redw/planyourvisit/conditions.htm
NPS current conditions page instructs visitors to check weather, wildfire/prescribed fire impacts, and AQI as the trip approaches. https://www.nps.gov/redw/planyourvisit/conditions.htm
Not promoted to gate-confirmed until the actual July 11 Little Bald Hills permit is confirmed and fire/weather/road checks are refreshed.
Redwood National and State Parks current conditions source checked July 2, 2026: https://www.nps.gov/redw/planyourvisit/conditions.htm
Redwood National and State Parks backcountry campsite source checked July 2, 2026: https://www.nps.gov/redw/planyourvisit/backcountry-campsites.htm
Redwood GPX water/camp milepoints measured locally July 2, 2026.

Near-route water/camp POIs: river access mile 5.3, Smith River mile 7.0, store 0.29 miles off route near mile 9.3, creek mile 9.5, Little Bald Hills camp and limited water mile 14.4, creek mile 18.5, creek crossing mile 20.9, river access mile 22.4.
Redwood NPS permit source says permits are online only, can be reserved same-day before 1pm and up to 180 days in advance, and often fill in summer.
California-priority subagent confirmed public GPX, terrain-map artifacts, Little Bald Hills bike access, and NPS backcountry permit requirement.
Treat Little Bald Hills as a dry camp unless a current official source says otherwise.
Official NPS current conditions page lists Little Bald Hills backcountry camp open and Bald Hills Road plus Howland Hill Road open as of the page's June 25, 2026 update.
NPS backcountry campsite page states Little Bald Hills allows hikers, bikers, and equestrians and that water must be packed in.
NPS permit page states backcountry permits are online only, often fill in summer, and are specific to place/date.
Proposed Redwood ride window: arrive July 10, ride Sand Camp/Paradise area to Little Bald Hills July 11, finish July 12, hold July 13 as buffer.
Local GPX water notes include river/creek access before and after camp, but Little Bald Hills itself must be treated as dry unless observed otherwise.

readinessEvidence.finding: NPS states backcountry camps can be reached by bike and lists specific bicycle/horseback regulations; route use remains subject to permit and current conditions.
All backcountry camping requires an online permit; the actual July 11 Little Bald Hills permit is not confirmed in this workspace.
Little Bald Hills is listed open and backcountry permits are online only.
NPS warns conditions change quickly and instructs visitors to check weather, wildfire/prescribed fire impacts, AQI, roads, and closures as the trip approaches.
NPS current conditions list Little Bald Hills backcountry camp as open.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: Redwood National and State Parks current conditions page

readinessEvidence.finding: NPS says free backcountry permits are required for overnight use of all backcountry camp areas.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: Redwood National and State Parks backcountry campsite page

readinessEvidence.finding: NPS describes Little Bald Hills Camp as a remote backcountry retreat for hikers, bikers, and equestrians.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: Redwood National and State Parks backcountry campsite page

readinessEvidence.finding: NPS campsite details for Little Bald Hills list five campsites and amenities, but water must be packed in.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: Redwood National and State Parks backcountry campsite page

readinessEvidence.finding: NPS current conditions page says conditions can change quickly due to weather, road maintenance, wildfires, and other factors; Bald Hills Road is open with hazard/road-construction delays.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: Redwood National and State Parks current conditions page

readinessEvidence.finding: NPS says backcountry permits are required year-round, online only, can be reserved same-day before 1pm and up to 180 days in advance, and often fill in summer.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: Redwood National and State Parks backcountry permit page

readinessEvidence.finding: Local GPX milepoint check found on/near-route water POIs before Little Bald Hills camp: river access at mile 5.3, Smith River at mile 7.0, creek at mile 9.5, and limited/seasonal water at camp mile 14.4.
The GPX waypoint at Little Bald Hills camp says limited water is seasonal/creek/faucet/trough and not guaranteed; carry overnight water or filter earlier reliable water.
Public GPX and source route exist for Redwood Backcountry Rambler.
NPS backcountry information supports Little Bald Hills as the intended bike-legal camp, with permit requirement and pack-in water caveat.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: Redwood National and State Parks backcountry pages

readinessEvidence.finding: NPS current conditions page lists Bald Hills Road open with hazard/construction delay notes, Howland Hill Road open, and Little Bald Hills backcountry camp open.
NPS campsite page says Little Bald Hills is for hikers, bikers, and equestrians, has 5 campsites, toilet/bear lockers/picnic tables/fire pits, and water must be packed in.
NPS permit page says backcountry permits are required year-round, online only, place/date specific, often fill in summer, and same-day reservations are possible before 1pm.
CAL FIRE current incident list does not show an active Del Norte or Humboldt incident in the statewide active-incident table, but this is not a substitute for departure-week fire/AQI refresh.
Little Bald Hills Camp is described as a remote backcountry retreat for hikers, bikers, and equestrians; campsite details list 5 campsites and required backcountry permit.
NPS lists Little Bald Hills water as pack-in-your-own, so public sources cannot clear the rider-specific overnight water carry/filter plan.

Backcountry permits are required year-round, online only, place/date specific, often fill in summer, and can be reserved same-day before 1pm and up to 180 days ahead; this does not confirm the actual July 11 Little Bald Hills permit.

Public permit page exposes the reservation entry point and says permit questions should use confirmation email/website, but the text view did not provide public July 11 Little Bald Hills availability or an issued permit confirmation.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: Redwood Parks Conservancy backcountry permit flow

readinessEvidence.finding: NPS lists Little Bald Hills backcountry camp open, Bald Hills Road open with hazard/road-construction notes and 20-30 minute delays, and Howland Hill Road open; NPS also warns conditions can change quickly.

CAL FIRE active statewide incident list showed Point, Range, Chloe, Sandy, Lucas, and Bridge fires, none in Del Norte or Humboldt counties; this is current fire evidence but not a substitute for July 8-10 refresh.
Official NWS forecast retrieved on July 2 does not cover the July 10-13 ride window, so target-date weather cannot be verified yet.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: NWS API forecast for Crescent City and inland Little Bald Hills/Gasquet vicinity

readinessEvidence.finding: AirNow public AQI was not captured as a date-specific July 10-13 verification; AQI gate remains uncleared.

Redwood Parks Conservancy exposes a backcountry camping scheduler including Little Bald Hills Backcountry Campsites, but exact July 11, 2026 permit availability was not visible as a simple public availability table/API in this pass.
NPS identifies Little Bald Hills Trail as a suggested bike route and Little Bald Hills Camp as designated for hikers, bicycles, horses, and pack animals; no e-bikes on Little Bald Hills Trail.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: Redwood National and State Parks bike and backcountry pages

readinessEvidence.finding: NPS current conditions, last updated June 25, 2026, list Little Bald Hills backcountry camp as open, but this is not an issued July 11 permit.
